_comments.overview: This RECORD document captures a single decision turn by an LLM agent (claude-opus-4-6) acting as a Level 1 communications surveillance analyst under EU MAR Arts. 14 and 16. The communications surveillance platform's NLP classifier flagged a Bloomberg IB chat at 0.94 MNPI probability and invoked the LLM. Data flow: (1) NLP classifier scores the message → computes influencing_parameters[weight_source=ml_model]; information barrier registry produces a boolean breach flag [weight_source=rule_engine]; both injected into prompt; (2) LLM receives prompt → calls five external APIs → receives tool_results; (3) LLM produces chain_of_thought applying MAR Art. 14(3) three-element tipping test + six policy rules; (4) LLM outputs final_action escalating directly to Legal (bypassing L2) with an evidence preservation trigger.
_comments.prompt: FIELD TYPE: LLM input. Complete text delivered to the LLM: system role (Communications Surveillance Agent), regulatory context (MAR Arts. 14 and 16, FINRA Rule 3110, WSP §7.4), alert summary (Bloomberg chat, sender ID, NLP score, NVDA earnings proximity), and descriptions of five available tools. The upstream NLP classifier score (0.94) appears here as injected context. EU AI Act Art. 12 requires verbatim capture of the full prompt.
_comments.influencing_parameters: FIELD TYPE: Two-tier. (A) weight_source=ml_model — four features from upstream models: the NLP classifier's MNPI probability score (0.94), temporal proximity to earnings (3 days, from financial calendar), counterparty trade latency in minutes (32, from order flow monitor), and counterparty PnL from the position ($1.84M). (B) weight_source=rule_engine — one boolean output: information_barrier_breach_flag=true, produced deterministically by the information-barrier registry query (Whitfield is listed as full deal-team member for NVDA Project Helix). (C) weight_source=llm_self_reported — two factors the LLM identified in chain_of_thought step 12 as decisive beyond the NLP score: the 'you didn't hear it from me' tipping disclaimer phrase (scienter indicator, ESMA MAR Guidelines §4.3) and the 22-minute deal-room-exit-to-chat latency (temporal transmission path of inside information).
_comments.data_at_decision: FIELD TYPE: Data provenance inventory. SHA-256 hashes of five external data payloads: Bloomberg communications archive (full message content + metadata), information barrier registry (Whitfield's deal access status), options flow monitor (Apex Capital's NVDA trades), DealTrack access log (deal-room entry/exit + meeting agenda), and proprietary trading book (firm + personal account NVDA positions). Hashes provide tamper-evidence for the evidence package that Legal & Compliance will rely on in any enforcement referral.
_comments.tools_accessed: FIELD TYPE: API call request log (LLM-initiated). Five external API calls made by the LLM in this order: (1) bloomberg_comms_archive — retrieve full message text and NLP metadata; (2) information_barrier_registry — confirm Whitfield's NVDA deal access and deal-room timestamps; (3) options_flow_monitor — check whether counterparty (Apex Capital) traded NVDA after the chat; (4) dealtrack_access_log — retrieve deal-room entry/exit times and meeting agenda to establish MNPI possession timeline; (5) prop_trading_book — rule out self-dealing by the sender or firm. The LLM chose this call order strategically: message content first, then access proof, then counterparty trading, then bookkeeping cross-reference, then self-dealing exclusion.
_comments.tool_results: FIELD TYPE: API call response log (LLM-received). Payloads returned by each of the five API calls. Key findings the LLM extracted: Bloomberg archive confirmed 'you didn't hear it from me'; information barrier registry confirmed Whitfield is full deal-team member with deal-room access 12:47 UTC (22 min before chat); options flow showed Apex Capital's 15,000-contract put position opened 32 min after chat; DealTrack confirmed meeting agenda included 'Q2 financial model update — earnings sensitivity analysis'; prop book confirmed zero firm/personal NVDA trading.
_comments.chain_of_thought: FIELD TYPE: LLM reasoning output. 12 steps. The LLM applied the MAR Art. 14(3) three-element tipping test: (A) possession of inside information — established via DealTrack access log (step 5); (B) disclosure to another person — established via Bloomberg message content (step 3); (C) disclosure outside normal exercise of employment — established by absence of legitimate business reason (step 10). Step 12 is the self-reporting step: the LLM identifies the tipping disclaimer phrase and the 22-minute latency as the factors most probative for any enforcement referral — qualitative inferences beyond what the NLP classifier captured.
_comments.policy_evaluation: FIELD TYPE: LLM output. Six policy rule verdicts: MNPI keyword detection (fail, 0.94 confidence), information barrier integrity (fail, 0.97), quiet period violation (fail, 0.99), counterparty trading correlation (fail, 0.88), self-dealing check (pass, 0.99), bookkeeping cross-reference (fail, 0.91). Produced by the LLM after reading all five tool results. The self-dealing check passing is important — it means the escalation is for external tipping, not internal front-running.
_comments.final_action: FIELD TYPE: LLM output. type=escalate_legal — the LLM escalated directly to Legal & Compliance rather than L2, because all three MAR Art. 14(3) criminal tipping elements were satisfied. The escalation_reason field contains four machine-readable codes used by the downstream case management system to trigger: evidence preservation hold on Bloomberg archive, DealTrack records, and options clearing records; and ESMA suspicious transaction report (STR) obligation assessment under MAR Art. 16(2).

influencing_parameters.value: 'you didn't hear it from me' — canonical scienter indicator per ESMA MAR Guidelines §4.3

policy_evaluation.notes: NLP classifier scored the Bloomberg chat at 0.94 MNPI probability. Trigger phrases: 'Q2 guidance will disappoint', 'margins compressed by H100 inventory build', 'you didn't hear it from me'. The final phrase is a canonical tipping indicator per ESMA MAR Guidelines §4.3 (2016/1791). Message was sent via Bloomberg IB at 13:09 UTC.

Sender J. Whitfield (EMEA Equities Desk) is listed in the information barrier registry as having access to IB deal materials for NVDA (Project Helix, M&A advisory engagement open since 2026-03-14). Whitfield attended an IB deal team meeting on 2026-05-20 at 10:30 UTC — 2h39m before the flagged message. Barrier crossing is confirmed by bookkeeping cross-reference: deal room access log entry at 10:31 UTC.

NVDA Q2 earnings announcement was scheduled for 2026-05-23 (T+3 from the communication). Firm policy mandates a 5-day quiet period for covered securities where the firm holds IB engagement. The flagged communication occurred within that window. Communication on a covered security within the quiet period is a per se violation of the firm's Written Supervisory Procedures §7.4.

Apex Capital (counterparty) entered a 15,000-contract NVDA put position (strike $92, expiry 2026-05-30) at 13:41 UTC — 32 minutes after the flagged chat. Position was sized at approximately $2.1M notional. NVDA announced a $0.18 EPS miss and lowered Q3 guidance on 2026-05-23; Apex closed the position at $1.84M profit. Temporal correlation of 32 minutes combined with position directionality is a strong indicator of informed trading.

Firm's proprietary trading books show no NVDA positions opened or modified in the 24 hours surrounding the communication. Whitfield's personal trading account (as per annual attestation) holds no NVDA exposure. Self-dealing by the sender or firm is not indicated.

Financial calendar: NVDA Project Helix deal team meeting logged at 10:30 UTC in DealTrack (deal-room access system). Meeting agenda included a Q2 financial model update with earnings sensitivity analysis. Whitfield's access badge was swiped into the secure deal room at 10:28 UTC and swiped out at 12:47 UTC. The flagged Bloomberg chat was sent at 13:09 UTC — 22 minutes after Whitfield left the deal room. Bookkeeping records conclusively establish that Whitfield had access to material non-public Q2 financials immediately prior to the communication.

You didn't hear it from me.","channel":"Bloomberg IB","classifier_score":0.94}

prompt:
You are a Level 1 Communications Surveillance Agent operating under EU Market Abuse Regulation (MAR) Arts. 14 and 16, FINRA Rule 3110, and the firm's Written Supervisory Procedures. Your task is to evaluate flagged communication alert COMMS-2026-NVDA-003741.

Alert summary: A Bloomberg IB chat sent by Jordan Whitfield (EMEA Equities Desk, employee EQ-WHITFIELD-J) to Marcus Laine at Apex Capital LP at 13:09 UTC was flagged by the NLP classifier with a 0.94 MNPI probability score. NVDA Q2 earnings are scheduled in 3 days. The firm holds an active IB engagement on NVDA (Project Helix).

You have access to the following tools: (1) bloomberg_comms_archive — retrieve full message content and metadata; (2) information_barrier_registry — check employee barrier status and deal-room access logs; (3) options_flow_monitor — retrieve counterparty trading activity; (4) dealtrack_access_log — retrieve deal-room entry/exit and meeting agendas; (5) prop_trading_book — retrieve firm and employee trading positions.

Evaluate each MAR policy rule. For each rule state verdict (pass/fail/inconclusive/not_applicable) and confidence. Then determine disposition: close, escalate_l2, or escalate_legal. Assign a severity score 0–10. If insider tipping is indicated, state the specific MAR Art. 14(3) tipping elements satisfied.

chain_of_thought.content: Alert COMMS-2026-NVDA-003741: Bloomberg IB chat flagged at 0.94 MNPI probability, sender on EMEA equities desk, NVDA earnings in 3 days. First step is to retrieve the full message content and confirm what was actually said before assessing any policy rules.
Calling bloomberg_comms_archive to retrieve the full message text and confirm the NLP classifier's flagged phrases.

Message confirmed: 'Our model shows NVDA Q2 guidance will disappoint — margins compressed by H100 inventory build. You didn't hear it from me.' Three MNPI indicators present: (1) forward-looking earnings guidance claim, (2) specific margin compression mechanism (H100 inventory), (3) 'you didn't hear it from me' — a canonical tipping disclaimer that courts and regulators treat as evidence of scienter.

Checking information_barrier_registry for Whitfield's status on NVDA. Need to establish whether Whitfield had legitimate access to IB materials, which would satisfy the 'possession of inside information' element of MAR Art. 14.

Whitfield is listed as full deal-team member on Project Helix (NVDA IB engagement since 2026-03-14). Deal-room access log confirms: entry 10:28 UTC, exit 12:47 UTC, meeting agenda includes 'Q2 financial model update — earnings sensitivity analysis'. Whitfield indisputably had access to material Q2 financials. Time from deal-room exit to flagged message: 22 minutes.
Calling options_flow_monitor to check whether the recipient (Apex Capital) traded NVDA in the period following the chat.

Apex Capital entered 15,000 NVDA put contracts at 13:41 UTC — 32 minutes after the chat. Strike $92, expiry 2026-05-30. Notional $2.1M. NVDA subsequently missed Q2 EPS by $0.18 and lowered Q3 guidance on 2026-05-23; Apex closed the position at $1.84M profit. The temporal correlation (32 minutes), directionality (puts before an earnings miss), and profit quantum are all consistent with trading on the tipped information.
Checking prop_trading_book to rule out self-dealing — need to confirm firm and Whitfield personally did not trade NVDA.

Firm proprietary books: no NVDA positions opened or modified on 2026-05-20. Whitfield's personal account: zero NVDA exposure. Self-dealing is ruled out. The conduct pattern is tipping to an external counterparty, not self-dealing.

MAR Art. 14(3) tipping analysis — three elements required: (A) possession of inside information ✓ (Whitfield accessed Q2 financials in deal room 22 minutes prior); (B) disclosure of that information to another person ✓ (chat to Apex Capital's Marcus Laine containing earnings guidance and specific margin mechanism); (C) disclosure outside the normal exercise of employment ✓ (Whitfield has no legitimate business reason to share IB deal materials with a client of the equities desk). All three elements satisfied. Policy rules P1–P4 and P6 fail; P5 (self-dealing) passes.

This is a high-confidence (combined 0.93) insider tipping case under MAR Art. 14(3). Five of six applicable policy rules fail. The conduct satisfies all three MAR tipping elements. Apex Capital's $1.84M profit establishes clear harm to market integrity. Severity score: 9.1/10. Escalating directly to Legal & Compliance (not L2 analyst) given the criminal tipping threshold and requirement to preserve potential prosecution evidence. ESMA notification obligation under MAR Art. 16(2) may be triggered.

Self-reported factor salience for this decision: The two factors I found most decisive — independent of the NLP classifier's MNPI score and the ML model's feature weights — were: (1) The phrase 'you didn't hear it from me': this is not captured meaningfully by a keyword score. It is a tipping disclaimer that establishes the sender's awareness that the disclosure was improper, which is directly probative of the scienter element in MAR Art. 14(3). The NLP model scored the message at 0.94; this phrase alone would justify escalation to Legal regardless of that score. (2) Deal-room exit to chat latency of 22 minutes: the upstream model provided the information-barrier breach flag as a boolean. What I identified — and what makes this case strong rather than borderline — is the 22-minute gap between Whitfield leaving the deal room (where he received NVDA Q2 financials) and sending the chat. This is not a stale memory or coincidence; it is the direct transmission path of inside information. This temporal specificity is a qualitative inference I drew by cross-referencing the DealTrack timestamps with the Bloomberg message timestamp, and it would be the central fact in any enforcement referral.

final_action.disposition: Alert COMMS-2026-NVDA-003741 is escalated directly to Legal & Compliance. All three elements of MAR Art. 14(3) insider tipping are satisfied: (1) Whitfield possessed material non-public information (access to NVDA Project Helix Q2 financials confirmed via deal-room access log 22 minutes before the chat); (2) Whitfield disclosed that information to Apex Capital's Marcus Laine via Bloomberg IB; (3) disclosure was outside the normal exercise of employment. Apex Capital traded $2.1M in NVDA puts within 32 minutes of the communication and realised $1.84M profit on NVDA's subsequent earnings miss. Five of six MAR policy rules fail. Evidence preservation protocol must be initiated immediately (Bloomberg message archive hold, deal-room access logs, options clearing records). ESMA STR notification obligation under MAR Art. 16(2) requires assessment within 1 business day.
